Class ModalitySideProofRule

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.rule.AbstractSideProofRule
de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.rule.ModalitySideProofRule
All Implemented Interfaces:
BuiltInRule, Rule, HasOrigin, Named, Rule, RuleExecutor<Goal>
public class ModalitySideProofRule extends AbstractSideProofRule

A BuiltInRule which evaluates a modality in a side proof.

This rule is applicable on top level terms (SequentFormula) of the form.

  • {...}\[...\](<something> = <var>) or
  • {...}\<...\>(<something> = <var>) or
  • {...}\[...\](<var> = <something>) or
  • {...}\<...\>(<var> = <something>)
The leading updates are optional and any JModality is supported.

The original SequentFormula which contains the equality is always removed in the following Goal. For each possible result value is a SequentFormula added to the Sequent of the form:

  • Antecedent: <resultCondition> -> <something> = <result> or
  • Antecedent: <resultCondition> -> <result> = <something> or
  • Succedent: <resultCondition> & <something> = <result> or
  • Succedent: <resultCondition> & <result> = <something>
The side proof uses the default side proof settings (splitting = delayed) and is started via SymbolicExecutionSideProofUtil.startSideProof(Proof, ProofEnvironment, Sequent). In case that at least one result branch has applicable rules an exception is thrown and the rule is aborted.

  • Field Details

    • INSTANCE

      public static final ModalitySideProofRule INSTANCE
      The singleton instance of this class.

  • Method Details

    • isApplicable

      public boolean isApplicable(Goal goal, PosInOccurrence pio)
      Returning true iff a rule is applicable at the given position. This does not necessarily mean that a rule application will change the goal (this decision is made due to performance reasons)

    • createApp

      public IBuiltInRuleApp createApp(PosInOccurrence pos, TermServices services)

    • apply

      public @NonNull ImmutableList<Goal> apply(Goal goal, RuleApp ruleApp) throws RuleAbortException
      applies the given rule application to the specified goal
      Parameters:
      goal - the goal that the rule application should refer to.
      ruleApp - the rule application that is executed.
      Returns:
      List of the goals created by the rule which have to be proved. If this is a close-goal-taclet ( this.closeGoal () ), the first goal of the return list is the goal that should be closed (with the constraint this taclet is applied under).
      Throws:
      RuleAbortException

    • name

      public Name name()
      returns the name of this element
      Returns:
      the name of the element

      public String displayName()
      returns the display name of the rule by default the name is the same as the rules unique name
